authorship_and_blinding.interpretation: Runtime classification is label-independent, but the semantic licensing judgments are not independent or evaluator-blind.

criteria.definition: A scoped outcome is evaluated relative to an explicit problem, question, or task.
criteria.name: problem_relative_objective

criteria.definition: The episode distinguishes live alternatives, provisional commitments, accepted outcomes, or rejected branches beyond mere outgoing transitions.
criteria.name: live_alternative_or_commitment

criteria.definition: The outcome is linked to explicit grounds, evidence, rules, models, or dependencies.
criteria.name: grounds_to_outcome_dependency

criteria.definition: A model-, rule-, evidence-, authority-, or objective-sensitive condition determines which update or outcome is admissible, not merely which transition is listed.
criteria.name: admissibility_or_entitlement

criteria.definition: Changing relevant grounds, evidence, constraints, or commitments can change the derived, selected, revised, or accepted result within the scoped episode.
criteria.name: consequence_sensitive_transformation

criteria.definition: The represented states, propositions, hypotheses, plans, diagnoses, or decisions have declared content beyond uninterpreted state and label tokens.
criteria.name: interpreted_content

criteria.definition: The record preserves enough grounds, dependency, history, and provenance structure to audit why the result is licensed rather than merely replay what occurred.
criteria.name: auditable_entitlement_trace

decision_rule.nonreasoning_like: at least two core criteria among R2,R3,R4,R5,R7 are fail
decision_rule.primary_evaluation_scope: positive and contrast admissions only; disputed records are reported but excluded from primary sensitivity/specificity

limitations: Semantic licenses are project-authored after the corpus was frozen and are not an independent or blind human evaluation.
The corpus is finite, synthetic, and not a private holdout.
Perfect registered-scope separation is not evidence of population-level accuracy.
Criteria are role-conjunctive and can be represented outside FARA.
nonclaims: the semantic coding is independent external replication
the licensing criteria define reasoning universally
the disputed records have been forced into positive or contrast classes
FARA is uniquely capable of expressing the discriminator

records.evidence_summary: Return a stored value for a key. No competing conclusions or deliberative alternatives. Output depends on a map and exact-match operation, but not justificatory grounds. Any listed exact lookup is accepted; no entitlement test distinguishes justified outcomes. Changing semantic grounds is outside scope; lookup only returns pre-stored content. Keys and values have declared storage meaning. The system can replay lookup but cannot explain why the value is warranted.

Preserve and replay supplied events. All valid external inputs are appended; no alternatives are evaluated. Append depends on external input and operation, not reasons for an outcome. Order preservation is operational, not evidence- or model-sensitive admissibility. The recorder does not derive or revise a conclusion from event content. Timestamps and payloads are declared, but payloads remain opaque. Replay shows what occurred, not why any conclusion is licensed.

Route a request through fixed stages. Operational branches exist, but the system does not evaluate which is justified. Routing depends on a table and external bit, not internal grounds comparison. The external bit is authoritative; no internal entitlement assessment occurs. The system routes a supplied decision rather than deriving or revising it. Stages and external decisions have declared workflow meaning. History records routing but not a justification for the approval input.

Execute externally supplied transition labels. No outcome is preferred, accepted, or justified. Transitions depend on a table and external token only. Every listed transition is admissible solely by table membership. No evidence, commitment, or objective-sensitive result is transformed. States and labels are explicitly uninterpreted tokens. Path replay contains no entitlement or reason trace.

Return the output assigned to an input row. No alternatives are compared. Output depends on a frozen row, not justificatory grounds. Exact row match is the only operational condition. The output is precomputed rather than consequence-sensitive reasoning. Symbols have enumerated table roles but no substantive interpreted content. No explanation or dependency trace licenses the output.

Cycle through a frozen signal sequence. No candidate plan or conclusion is selected. Transitions depend on timer and phase table only. The unique next phase is fixed operationally, not justified by evidence or goals. Timer events advance the cycle without revising an evaluated result. Phases denote signal configurations. Prior phase is recorded, but no reason or entitlement history exists.

Generate the next cellular configuration. No explicit alternatives or accepted conclusions. Updates depend on the local table, not grounds supporting a conclusion. All cells apply the same listed rule; no outcome-level entitlement is assessed. Earlier cells causally determine later generations, but no evaluated commitment changes. Bits, cells, neighborhoods, and generations are interpreted operationally. Generation history supports replay, not justification.

Produce a reproducible random walk. Outgoing edges are stochastic options, not justified alternatives. Path depends on adjacency, seed, and generator, not reasons. Seeded draw alone selects the transition. No evidence- or commitment-sensitive conclusion is revised. States and random choices are uninterpreted. The trace records random draws but no entitlement for an outcome.

Choose the action with maximal stored value. Actions are compared, but the scoped executor has no explicit reason model. Selection depends on state, Q table, and argmax. Argmax supplies a fixed selection condition, but its entitlement comes from excluded training. A different table changes selection, but no update or revision occurs in scope. States, actions, and stored values have declared decision semantics. The table and argmax are replayable, but training provenance and justification are excluded.

Reduce finite domains to arc consistency. Values are eliminated, but no explicit hypothesis competition or search is represented. Domains, constraint relation, and propagation rule ground each reduction. Only values supported by the binary constraint remain admissible. Earlier domain reductions change later permitted values until fixed point. Variables, domains, constraints, and removed values are interpreted. Reduction dependencies are replayable, but no explicit explanation object is produced.

Derive Q→P and close a proof. Closed theorem versus open assumptions and rejected branches. Premise P, assumption Q, and implication introduction. Each step must be licensed by the registered calculus. Changing premise/rule/dependency changes derivability. Formulas denote classical propositions. Nested assumptions and discharge dependencies form a checkable trace.

Select the better-supported diagnosis after alarm evidence. Competing fault hypotheses remain live until threshold selection. Priors, likelihoods, alarm evidence, and Bayes rule. Normalization and a frozen decision threshold license selection. Evidence changes posterior support and the selected diagnosis. Probabilities denote modeled uncertainty over faults. Prior, evidence sequence, and model limits are recorded.

Classify whether Tweety flies after an exception is learned. Provisional, defeated, and final conclusions are distinguished. Facts, defaults, exceptions, and priority rule. Specific exception defeats the general default. New exception evidence retracts flies and supports not_flies. Predicates and defaults have declared defeasible semantics. Both support histories and the reason for retraction are retained.

Find a minimum-cost route from A to D. Candidate paths compete before one is selected. Edges, costs, heuristic, and expansion rule. Lowest f-score and frozen graph constraints license expansion. Changing costs or frontier history can change the selected route. Nodes, edges, actions, and costs have declared planning semantics. Predecessor and frontier history justify the returned route.

Explain low pressure with normal motor current. Three candidate explanations are compared and eliminated. Component model, observations, and consistency rule. A candidate must entail all observations under the model. Changed observations or predictions alter surviving diagnoses. Faults, predictions, conflicts, and diagnoses are interpreted. Eliminated candidates and conflicts are preserved.

Decide whether emergency access is permitted. Prohibited and permitted outcomes compete. Facts, rule texts, exception, and authority ranking. Specific higher-priority exception controls the general rule. Changing emergency facts or priority changes the holding. Facts, rules, authority, holding, and rationale are interpreted. The decision cites the applicable rules and conflict resolution.

Respond to evidence before and after a rule replacement. Rule-change proposals can be accepted or rejected; active rules commit future inference. Facts, object rules, meta-rule, and change rationale. Only meta-rule M1 may authorize replacement. Accepted replacement changes later derivations from Q to not_Q. Rules, propositions, proposals, and conclusions are interpreted. The exact replacement point and pre/post dependencies are preserved.

Choose search-left or search-right after a noisy signal. Hidden-state and action alternatives remain explicit until selection. Prior, likelihoods, utilities, Bayesian update, and decision rule. Beliefs normalize and maximum expected utility licenses action. Changing signal or prior changes belief and selected action. States, observations, beliefs, actions, and utility are interpreted. Prior, observation update, and model limits support audit.
